rationale: The actual thread shows this issue is still unresolved — yenuo26 pings Flink-ddd, and Flink-ddd explicitly asks yenuo26 to trigger a full CI sleep-mode test run, i.e. no diagnosis or fix has been confirmed. Both candidates instead confidently assert the root cause (PR #4834's guard), claim the fix (level=2→level=1) is 'already applied on main,' and recommend closing — a confident answer contradicted by the thread's own state, hurting correctness for both similarly. Y is weaker on grounding: it fabricates a specific quoted sentence from the PR #4834 body that can't be verified, and its own diagnostics show tests_run is empty yet the draft claims the test 'passes on current main (verified at the revision in this checkout)' — an unsupported verification claim. X makes the same core overreach but without inventing a quote or a false verification claim, and adds slightly more complete runtime guidance, giving it a marginal edge.
